rationale: CCC Domain D ("Curricular Meta-Modeling") and DigCompEdu 2.2 ("Creating and modifying digital content") both concern authoring, not merely consuming, digital material. DigCompEdu 2.2 is framed around general digital teaching content (documents, media, learning resources); CCC Domain D is specifically about contributing to and modifying machine-readable curriculum knowledge structures themselves (concept entries, learning progression models, alignment records) -- a much more structurally constrained authoring task than DigCompEdu 2.2 anticipates. relatedMatch reflects that CCC Domain D is meaningfully harder and more specialized than a narrowing of DigCompEdu 2.2 would suggest; it is not simply "content creation, but for curricula."
subject: CCC-v1.0.0:OE-COMPETENCY-001015
